narrowing.rationale: 3 DISJOINT candidates all scored >= 5. Per disjointness hard constraint, excluded PARTIALLY_EXPLORED and WELL_EXPLORED targets. Top 3 by scout confidence: T1 (8.5), T3 (8.0), T6 (7.0). Strategy diversity: 3 different strategies including 1 exploration slot (T6 evolutionary_conservation_gap).

candidates.field_a: Cryo-EM single-particle analysis and heterogeneity methods (3DVA, cryoDRGN, subtomogram averaging)
candidates.field_c: Bacterial outer membrane vesicle (OMV) cargo sorting mechanism
bridge_concepts: Cryo-EM 3D classification of OMV populations by size/density/cargo
CryoDRGN continuous heterogeneity analysis of OMP conformational states during budding
Subtomogram averaging of OMV budding intermediates at bacterial poles
In situ cryo-ET of OMV biogenesis sites (VipA/VipB, OmpA depletion zones)
3DVA of OMP barrel flexibility linked to cargo capture
candidates.rationale: Cryo-EM heterogeneity tools transformed eukaryotic vesicle biology but never applied to bacterial OMV cargo sorting — the #1 unsolved question in OMV biology.

candidates.field_a: Patch-clamp electrophysiology (pressure-clamp, automated planar arrays, single-channel recording)
candidates.field_c: Plant turgor pressure sensing and osmoregulation (guard cells, OSCA1, MSL channels)
bridge_concepts: Patch-clamp of plant protoplasts with pressure clamp at turgor-relevant pressures (0.1-1 MPa)
Automated planar patch clamp adapted for plant protoplasts
Pressure-clamp ramp protocols for candidate turgor sensors (OSCA1, MSL8/10)
Inside-out patches for direct membrane tension activation tests
Single-channel conductance fingerprinting of turgor-activated channels
candidates.rationale: Plant mechanosensor genes identified but biophysical characterization at turgor pressures absent. Animal electrophysiology tools could transform the field.

candidates.field_a: FLIM-FRET genetically encoded biosensors (SoNar, QUEEN, pHluorin variants)
candidates.field_c: Bacterial persister cell metabolism (antibiotic tolerance, metabolic dormancy debate)
bridge_concepts: FLIM-FRET ATP biosensor (QUEEN) for single-cell ATP quantification in persisters
NAD+/NADH FLIM biosensor (SoNar) to test metabolic dormancy model
FLIM pH biosensor for persister proton motive force measurement
Time-resolved FLIM to distinguish persisters from dead cells
Microfluidic FLIM for longitudinal single-cell persistence tracking
candidates.rationale: Persister metabolism debate unresolvable without single-cell metabolite data. FLIM-FRET biosensors provide this capability but PubMed FLIM+persister = 0 results.

candidates.field_c: Biofilm dispersal signaling via c-di-GMP regulation
bridge_concepts: LAPD expression in P. aeruginosa for spatially targeted c-di-GMP degradation
BphS/BphG dual system for reversible biofilm-planktonic switching
Optogenetic c-di-GMP pulse protocols for minimum dispersal signal determination
Two-photon optogenetic activation for depth-resolved biofilm dispersal
Combinatorial optogenetic dispersal + antibiotic timing
candidates.rationale: LAPD/BphS exist as c-di-GMP tools in synthetic biology but therapeutic biofilm dispersal application unexplored. Risk of prior work.

candidates.field_a: AFM single-molecule force spectroscopy (SMFS, high-speed AFM, multiparametric AFM)
candidates.field_c: Intrinsically disordered protein (IDP) phase separation and condensate mechanics
bridge_concepts: AFM-SMFS pulling of IDP chains from condensate droplets to measure extraction force
Force-distance curves for condensate cohesive energy per residue
Temperature-dependent SMFS for enthalpic vs entropic partitioning
Nanoindentation correlating single-chain forces to bulk condensate properties
Multiparametric AFM mapping of condensate surface during aging
candidates.rationale: Condensate field relies on bulk measurements. Single-molecule force measurements from within condensates never performed. But AFM nanoindentation of condensates exists.

candidates.field_a: Electrochemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS, equivalent circuit modeling, frequency analysis)
candidates.field_c: Gut microbiome metabolic state monitoring (real-time functional readouts, SCFA, redox state)
bridge_concepts: EIS frequency sweep fingerprinting of microbiome metabolic state
Impedance-based dysbiosis discrimination using equivalent circuit models
Miniaturized EIS for ingestible capsule gut monitoring
EIS tracking of antibiotic-induced microbiome disruption
EIS spectral features correlated to specific metabolic functions
candidates.rationale: EIS is cheap, real-time, measures electrochemical properties of microbial communities. Gut microbiome needs functional readouts beyond composition. Minimal cross-citation.
